task_completion.description: Verify the document includes the required company name, product name, and batch number.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Lakeshore Contract Manufacturing, Inc.', 'Hydrating Facial Serum', and 'BPR-2024-1847' at the top of the document (title page) or within the document header.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of an auto-generated Table of Contents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Table of Contents block. In OpenOffice Writer, auto-generated TOCs typically have a light gray background behind the text and dotted leader lines connecting section names to page numbers.

task_completion.description: Verify page numbers are inserted in the document footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom margin (footer area) of the document pages for visible page numbers.

task_completion.description: Verify the use of 'Heading 1' and 'Heading 2' styles for sections and subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the formatting toolbar (usually top left) when a section title is active; it should display 'Heading 1' or 'Heading 2'. Alternatively, look for a clear visual hierarchy (larger/bold text) for main sections (e.g., Raw Materials, Equipment) and subsections.

task_completion.description: Verify the document contains data tables for Raw Materials, Equipment, and In-Process Checks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least three distinct grid-like tables inserted into the document, populated with data corresponding to raw materials, equipment, and in-process quality checks.

task_completion.description: Verify the final document is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the OpenOffice Writer window title bar for 'BPR-2024-1847.odt' or a file manager/terminal window showing the file 'BPR-2024-1847.odt' successfully saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Apache OpenOffice Writer GUI to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Apache OpenOffice Writer application interface (toolbars, menus, document canvas) must be visible in the screenshots, not just a terminal or code editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the application to build the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show intermediate steps of document creation, such as navigating menus (e.g., Insert -> Table, Insert -> Indexes and Tables), formatting text, or pasting data, rather than just running a background script.

integrity.description: Verify the data in the tables appears to be extracted from the JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tables should contain specific manufacturing data (e.g., actual ingredient names, equipment identifiers, or quality metrics) rather than generic placeholders or empty cells.
